checks.a_analysis: The output JSON contains text that is entirely derived from the source document. All headings, list items, and names (Roy Cooper, James A. Weaver, etc.) are present in the original PDF. No extraneous content, introductions, or summaries were added beyond the permitted AI-generated metadata and alt text.
There are no phone numbers, email addresses, or physical addresses in the source document. The output correctly reflects this absence and does not contain any fabricated contact information.

The source document consists of a single page containing a header and an agenda list. The output JSON includes both the header information (Governor and Secretary details) and the complete agenda structure with its nested sub-items. No sections were omitted.
The source document contains no tables; therefore, no rows are missing in the output.

checks.a_analysis: All text from the source, including the specific phrasing 'Revised: Next Steps for Each Sub-Committee (March 2024ff)' and the sub-committee names, is captured accurately. The hierarchical structure of the agenda is preserved in the list format.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: The text content is highly accurate. For instance, the unusual notation '(March 2024ff)' in the source is correctly transcribed in the output. No semantic shifts or incorrect dates/times were identified.

checks.a_analysis: Each piece of text from the source document (header info, titles, agenda items) appears exactly once in the output JSON. There are no duplicated paragraphs or list items.

The heading hierarchy is logical and follows the visual structure of the document. The main document title is used as a section title (H1 level equivalent), followed by an H2 for the meeting name and an H3 for 'AGENDA'. No levels are skipped.
The source document contains no tables.

The reading order in the JSON follows the top-to-bottom, left-to-right flow of the source document. It correctly processes the header information first, then the meeting titles, then the agenda items.

The agenda is correctly captured as a nested unordered list. The primary bullet points match the source, and the sub-bullets under 'Next Steps for Each Sub-Committee' are correctly nested as a sub-list within the parent list item.
The source document contains no hyperlinks, and none were incorrectly injected into the output.

checks.a_analysis: The HTML output maintains the visual organization of the source document. The header information, document titles, and the structured agenda with bullets and sub-bullets are presented in the same logical sequence. While there are minor differences in alignment (the source is centered, the HTML is left-aligned) and the placement of header metadata, the overall organization of information remains consistent and easy to find.
The source PDF does not contain any tables.
The NCDIT logo, which is the only meaningful image in the source document, is present and correctly rendered in the HTML output.

The header information containing the Governor's and Secretary's names and titles is visually duplicated in the HTML output. In the source PDF, this text appears only once in the top-right corner. In the HTML screenshot, the same text blocks appear twice: once on the left side under the logo and once on the right side.
checks.b_evidence_source: "Roy Cooper Governor" and "James A. Weaver Secretary and State Chief Information Officer" appearing once in the header area.
checks.b_evidence_output: "Roy Cooper Governor" and "James A. Weaver Secretary and State Chief Information Officer" are duplicated within the 'Header' section; once left-aligned and once right-aligned.
